tickets.title: Fix flaky test: FixtureEngine.loadFixtures must tolerate concurrent/partial fixture files (shared decisions dir race)

tickets.context:
Baseline (oracle.md): bun run check + build + test pass KEYLESS. Touch ONLY declared files. Add a test for the new behavior. RUNTIME v2-017: server under Node/tsx.

FLAKY-TEST DEFECT (measured, stack-traced): test/server.test.ts and test/api.tailor-db.test.ts each writeFileSync/rmSync temp .json fixtures into the SHARED dir test/fixtures/decisions/ from SEPARATE parallel vitest workers. FixtureEngine.loadFixtures (src/server/tailor/engine.ts ~lines 82-90) does readdirSync(dir).filter(.json).map(f => JSON.parse(readFileSync(...))) â€” it reads+parses EVERY .json with NO per-file guard, so when a worker catches another worker's file mid-write (partial/empty) or mid-delete (ENOENT), JSON.parse/readFileSync throws, the whole map() throws, decide() throws, and /api/tailor returns 502 instead of 200. Return the successfully-parsed fixtures. A mid-write/mid-delete concurrent file becomes a no-op for other workers, while a test's own fully-written fixture is still found by key. Do NOT change decide()/hashKey/matching semantics; do NOT touch the tests that write fixtures (the fix is in the engine). Keep DEFAULT_FIXTURES_DIR behavior.
